title: Dev foundation: secrets hygiene (detect-secrets + .env.example)
description: Добавить защиту от случайного коммита ключей/секретов (особенно OpenAI/GCP), т.к. CI нет и всё держится на локальных гейтах.
design:
Используем detect-secrets как pre-commit hook.
Коммитим `.secrets.baseline` и документируем команду обновления.
По умолчанию сканируем весь репозиторий, исключаем только очевидные артефакты (например, `uv.lock`, `.beads/`, `*.db`).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] В репозитории есть `.env.example` с перечнем нужных переменных без реальных значений.
- [ ] Реальные файлы `.env` и другие секреты (ключи, json credentials) гарантированно не попадают в git (gitignore).
- [ ] Настроен `detect-secrets` (baseline + pre-commit hook), и `pre-commit` блокирует добавление секретов.
- [ ] Есть короткая инструкция в `README.md`, как обновлять baseline при ложных срабатываниях.
notes:
COMPLETED:
- Added detect-secrets dev dependency (uv add --dev detect-secrets)
- Added .env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, LOG_LEVEL
- Generated .secrets.baseline with excludes (.beads, .venv, uv.lock, *.db)
- Added .pre-commit-config.yaml with detect-secrets hook
- Updated README with scan command + baseline update instructions
- Verified:  and Detect secrets.......................................(no files to check)Skipped

title: Runtime foundation: structured logging + request correlation
description:
Сделать единый слой логирования для прототипа, чтобы диагностика была предсказуемой и пригодной для продакшен-подхода.

Требования: структурированные логи, корреляция событий одного действия пользователя (request_id), привязка к document_id, безопасный вывод без секретов.
design:
Минимальный стек: стандартный `logging` + `contextvars`.

Подход:
- `context.py`: contextvars для `request_id`, `document_id` + context manager `request_context(...)`.
- `logging_setup.py`: `logging.config` + Filter, который подставляет контекстные поля в каждый LogRecord.
- Для внешних вызовов: helper `timed_call(name, fn, **fields)` или декоратор, который пишет duration и ошибки.

Формат: по умолчанию logfmt (читаемо как CLI), опционально можно добавить переключатель на JSON.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Логирование настраивается в одном месте (например, `logging_setup.py`) и используется всем приложением.
- [ ] Формат логов структурированный (logfmt или JSON) и включает минимум: timestamp, level, event/message, request_id (если есть), document_id (если есть).
- [ ] Уровень логирования управляется через env (`LOG_LEVEL`).
- [ ] Обёртки внешних вызовов (OpenAI/GCP) логируют start/end + duration + ошибки без утечки ключей/секретов.
- [ ] В Gradio обработчиках на каждое действие генерируется `request_id`, и логи внутри действия содержат этот id.
- [ ] Есть unit-тест, что контекстные поля (request_id/document_id) реально попадают в лог-запись.
notes: Implemented contextvars-based logging foundation (context, logging setup, timed_call), added tests for context fields + redaction, wired app startup to configure logging. Tests/ruff/pyright pass.
close_reason: Logging foundation complete; Gradio wiring will use request_context/new_request_id in dm-dhq.7

title: Runtime foundation: timeouts/retries/backoff (OpenAI + GCP Vision)
description:
Сделать надёжные внешние вызовы к OpenAI и Google Vision: явные таймауты, ретраи с backoff+jitter, обработка rate limit/5xx, понятные ошибки в UI.

Так как CI нет, это должно быть легко проверяемо локально и хорошо логироваться.
design:
Реализовать общий слой:
- `retry.py`: классификация retryable ошибок + backoff (желательно `tenacity`).
- `openai_client.py`: обёртка над OpenAI SDK с timeout + retry.
- `vision_client.py`: обёртка над `document_text_detection` с timeout + retry.

Семантика:
- Не ретраим 4xx кроме 408/429.
- Для 429 учитываем `Retry-After`, если доступно, иначе backoff.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Все внешние вызовы к OpenAI и GCP Vision имеют явный timeout (нет бесконечных зависаний).
- [ ] Есть единая retry-политика: ретраим только транзиентные ошибки (сетевые, 429, 5xx/timeout), с exponential backoff + jitter и max attempts.
- [ ] Параметры таймаутов/ретраев настраиваются через env (например `OPENAI_TIMEOUT_S`, `GCP_TIMEOUT_S`, `RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS`).
- [ ] Ошибки пробрасываются в UI как короткое человеко-понятное сообщение + рекомендация "повторить" (без падения приложения).
- [ ] Логи содержат start/end + duration + попытку ретрая + request_id, без утечки секретов.
- [ ] Есть минимум 1 unit/smoke тест на retry-классификацию (ретраим/не ретраим).
notes: Added retry module with backoff/jitter, retryable classification incl. gRPC mapping, and ExternalServiceError. Added OpenAI/Vision client wrappers with retries/timeouts + user-friendly errors, updated config/env/docs for retry params, and tests for retry + config.
close_reason: Retry/timeout foundation complete; wrappers ready for OCR/LLM modules

title: Prototype language: English-only UI + prompts
description: Translate all user-facing strings to English (UI labels/status messages, chat/summarizer prompts, retry/user-facing errors). Default LLM output language to English for the prototype.
design: Fast path: hardcode English defaults and translate strings; no i18n framework for prototype.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] UI labels/messages are English-only.
- [x] Summarizer + chat prompts instruct English output by default.
- [x] User-facing error messages (OCR/OpenAI/retry) are English.
- [x] Tests updated for new English strings.
notes:
Translated UI labels/status messages + prompts to English, switched default language to en, updated error messages.
Tests: ruff check, pyright, pytest.

title: Fix chat: long OCR docs show as no-data
description: Chat answers always 'No data in the provided documents.' after Force OCR because chat context falls back to a dummy summary for long docs. Fix context building so long docs use existing stored summary if present, otherwise include a truncated excerpt of OCR text (no dummy).
design: For docs where text length > CHAT_DOC_MAX_CHARS: use latest stored doc summary (type=doc) if available; else include an excerpt of OCR text (first N chars). Avoid LLM calls during context building; keep chat responsive.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] For long documents without a stored summary, chat context includes an excerpt (not dummy text).
- [x] If a doc summary exists, chat context uses it.
- [x] Added unit tests for both paths.
notes:
Root cause: chat context used a dummy summary when OCR text exceeded CHAT_DOC_MAX_CHARS, leading to "No data" answers.
Fix: prefer stored doc summary, otherwise include truncated excerpt of OCR text (no dummy/LLM call during context build).
Tests: added unit tests and ran pytest.

title: SQLite: схема и слой хранения (documents/pages/summaries/messages)
description: Реализовать хранение "памяти" прототипа в SQLite: тексты страниц, саммари, история чата, связи.
design: Простой слой `storage.py`: функции upsert/get/list. Без ORM, чистый sqlite3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] При первом запуске создаются таблицы без ручных миграций.
- [ ] Можно сохранить документ (метаданные + текст по страницам) и затем прочитать обратно.
- [ ] Можно сохранять сообщения чата и связи "сообщение ↔ документ(ы)".
- [ ] Есть unit-тесты на основные операции `storage` (documents/pages/messages/message_documents).
notes: Expanded SQLite schema to documents/pages/summaries/messages/message_documents with checks + indexes. Added storage helpers (save/load docs, save/list messages, save/get/list summaries) and updated TDD tests.
close_reason: SQLite schema and storage layer complete with tests

title: Chat: ignore placeholder doc summaries
description: Older versions created doc summaries with placeholder text (e.g., 'No data in the provided documents.' / 'Нет данных...'). Chat context currently prefers stored summaries for long docs, which can keep Q&A stuck on the placeholder. Detect and ignore placeholder summaries, falling back to OCR excerpt.
design: When selecting latest doc summary, treat known placeholder strings as invalid (whitespace/punctuation-insensitive).
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] If latest stored doc summary is placeholder, chat context uses excerpt instead.
- [x] Unit test covers this.
notes:
Added placeholder-summary detection (EN/RU) so chat ignores dummy summaries created by older versions and falls back to OCR excerpt.
Tests: ruff, pyright, pytest.

title: UI: make OCR rerunnable by resolving upload path
description: Force OCR sometimes does nothing because UI only knows PDF paths from in-memory uploads_state; after restart or state reset it can't find the PDF and silently skips OCR (only status message). Make parse/OCR resolve the deterministic path data/uploads/<doc_id>.pdf so OCR runs and Vision calls appear in logs.
design: Introduce small helper module for upload path resolution (deterministic doc_id.pdf). UI uses resolve_upload_pdf_path() and discard_upload_pdf(). Add unit tests for helper. Keep behavior: delete PDF after successful OCR or after parse when OCR not needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] Force OCR works after app restart if data/uploads/<doc_id>.pdf exists.
- [x] Unit tests cover resolve/discard behavior.
- [x] Gradio css warning removed by passing css to launch().
notes:
Added upload path helpers (deterministic <uploads_dir>/<doc_id>.pdf) and updated UI parse/OCR to resolve path even if uploads_state lost (restart).
Moved Gradio css to launch() to match Gradio 6.
Tests: ruff, pyright, pytest.

title: OCR UX: don't auto-delete uploads; stronger OCR-needed heuristic + logs
description: Users don't see Vision calls and OCR often doesn't run because PDFs get deleted after Parse/Re-parse. Also OCR-needed heuristic is too weak for scanned docs with small extracted text. Keep uploaded PDFs until explicit cleanup, use a stronger heuristic to mark docs as OCR-needed, and add explicit OCR start/end logs + show extracted char counts in UI status.
design:
- Stop deleting PDFs automatically on parse/OCR; add a cleanup button to delete selected uploads.
- In handle_parse use needs_ocr_pages(..., min_chars=2000) to flag OCR-needed.
- Add logger.info around OCR start/end; include dpi/lang hints and total extracted chars.
- Keep VisionClient call_with_retry logs as the primary indicator of actual Vision API calls.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] After Parse, user can always run Force OCR without re-upload.
- [x] Scanned PDFs with small extracted text get status OCR (not OK).
- [x] Logs show ocr.start/ocr.end and vision.document_text_detection events when OCR runs.
- [x] UI status message includes total extracted chars.
notes:
UI changes:
- Parse uses needs_ocr_pages(..., min_chars=2000)
- Upload PDFs are kept until explicit cleanup
- Added "Delete uploaded PDFs" button
- Added ocr.start/ocr.end + per-doc logs and status message with extracted char counts

Tests: ruff, pyright, pytest.

title: OpenAI: configurable reasoning effort (default low for gpt-5*)
description: Support 'thinking low' for gpt-5-nano by sending Responses API reasoning.effort. This reduces latency/cost and may avoid timeouts.
design: Add optional env var OPENAI_REASONING_EFFORT (none|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh). Pass payload['reasoning']={effort:...} in OpenAIClient for gpt-5* models when configured. Add unit test via monkeypatching _build_client.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] OPENAI_REASONING_EFFORT is loaded from env and validated.
- [x] OpenAIClient includes reasoning.effort in Responses requests for gpt-5* models.
- [x] Unit test verifies payload includes reasoning.effort.
notes:
Added Config.openai_reasoning_effort (env OPENAI_REASONING_EFFORT; defaults to low for gpt-5*).
OpenAIClient now injects reasoning.effort for gpt-5* models.
Updated README/.env.example and added unit tests.
Tests: ruff, pyright, pytest.

title: OCR: tile-based Vision OCR for low-quality scans
description: Implement robust OCR for noisy multi-column scans by running Google Vision OCR on page tiles (grid) in reading order, then assembling text. This should dramatically increase extracted text for newspaper scans.
design: Use PyMuPDF clip rendering to generate tile images (normalized grid + small overlap, optional margin trim). For Arabic, order columns right-to-left. Trigger tile OCR when full-page OCR char_count is below a threshold. Store tile metadata in ocr_meta and add explicit logs for tile mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Force OCR on low-quality scans uses tile mode when full-page OCR is weak
- [ ] Extracted chars increases significantly vs full-page OCR (debug metric)
- [ ] Tile reading order is correct for ar (RTL)
- [ ] Unit tests cover tile grid generation + ordering + clip rendering returns bytes
notes:
Implemented tile OCR (Vision) for low-quality scans:
- pdf_ingest.render_page_png supports clip
- ocr.build_tile_clips (RTL-aware) + ocr_page_tiles_with_vision
- ocr_pdf_pages_robust switches to tile mode when full-page OCR is weak
- UI Force OCR uses ocr_pdf_pages_robust
- Tests cover clip rendering + tile ordering

title: OCR: OpenAI vision fallback when Vision is weak
description: When Google Vision (even with tiling) produces very little text, fall back to OpenAI vision OCR to get a usable transcript for chat/summaries.
design: Use Responses API with input_image (base64 data URLs) and a strict OCR prompt (no translation, output only text). Add OPENAI_VISION_MODEL env (default gpt-4.1-mini) and call OpenAI only when OCR quality is below threshold. Log fallback usage and store in ocr_meta.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] If Vision OCR char_count below threshold, system calls OpenAI vision OCR
- [ ] OPENAI_VISION_MODEL configurable via env (default gpt-4.1-mini)
- [ ] Unit tests mock OpenAIClient and verify image payload + prompt
- [ ] UI/logs clearly show when fallback is used
notes:
Implemented OpenAI vision fallback OCR:
- Added Config.openai_vision_model (env OPENAI_VISION_MODEL; defaults to gpt-4.1-mini unless OPENAI_MODEL is gpt-5*)
- Added docmind/openai_vision_ocr.py to call Responses API with input_image (base64 data URLs)
- Extended ocr_pdf_pages_robust to call OpenAI when tiled Vision OCR is still weak
- UI Force OCR now passes openai_client + config.openai_vision_model
- Added unit tests for payload + robust fallback

title: Chat: vision fallback for layout/structure questions
description: When chat cannot answer from OCR text (returns 'No data in the provided documents.'), automatically fall back to OpenAI vision Q&A using rendered page images (tiles) so users can ask layout/structure questions like 'How many articles?' reliably.
design: In chat_engine.answer_question: after text-based answer, if answer is placeholder or question matches layout keywords, attempt vision fallback when exactly one doc selected and PDF exists at data/uploads/<doc_id>.pdf. Render tiles (RTL aware) via pdf_ingest.render_page_png(clip=...). Call Responses API with input_image tiles using OPENAI_VISION_MODEL and a strict prompt. Log chat.vision_fallback.*.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Asking 'How many articles?' returns a non-empty answer for scanned pages\n- [ ] If PDF missing, fallback skipped with clear message\n- [ ] Unit tests cover fallback trigger + image payload assembly
notes: Implemented chat OpenAI-vision fallback for layout/count questions when text-only chat returns placeholder. Uses page tiles from uploaded PDF (data/uploads/<doc_id>.pdf) and OPENAI_VISION_MODEL. Added unit tests.

title: Config: default OPENAI_VISION_MODEL to a vision-capable model
description: Avoid defaulting OPENAI_VISION_MODEL to OPENAI_MODEL for gpt-5* (may not be vision-capable). Default to gpt-4.1-mini unless explicitly set.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] If OPENAI_VISION_MODEL unset, config.openai_vision_model == 'gpt-4.1-mini' even when OPENAI_MODEL is gpt-5-nano\n- [ ] Unit test covers this
notes: Changed default OPENAI_VISION_MODEL to gpt-4.1-mini (vision-capable) regardless of OPENAI_MODEL. Added test for gpt-5-nano case.

title: UI: preserve original filename and show correct page count on upload
description: After Parse/OCR the document title gets overwritten with '<doc_id>.pdf' (because we save title from uploads path). Also newly uploaded multi-page PDFs show pages=0 until manual Parse. Preserve original filename and set correct page count immediately on upload.
design: - Store original filename in documents.title and never overwrite with upload path basename.\n- On upload, extract PDF text layer (fast) to populate pages and set status (OCR/OK), so page_count shows real value.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] After Force OCR / Parse, titles remain the original filename\n- [ ] After upload, page_count reflects actual PDF page count (not 0)
close_reason: Preserve original document titles across Parse/OCR and populate page_count on upload.

title: UI: deduplicate uploads by file hash
description: Uploading the same PDF twice creates duplicate documents (new doc_id each time). Deduplicate by hashing file bytes and reusing existing document record.
design: Add documents.file_hash column with migration in init_db; unique index; handle_upload computes SHA256 and reuses existing doc_id when hash matches.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Uploading the same PDF twice results in a single document in the list\n- [ ] Existing docs continue to work after DB migration\n- [ ] Tests cover file_hash lookup + migration
close_reason: Deduplicate uploads by SHA256 file hash (DB migration + lookup).

title: PDF ingest: извлечение текста + детектор needs_ocr (PyMuPDF)
description: Сделать импорт PDF: извлечение текста по страницам и определение случаев, когда нужен OCR.
design:
PyMuPDF (fitz): `page.get_text()` для текста, `page.get_pixmap(dpi=...)` для рендера.
Порог needs_ocr: по количеству букв/слов на странице.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Для text-PDF извлекается текст по страницам (page_count, text_char_count).
- [ ] Для image-PDF (скан) детектор помечает `needs_ocr` (например, если текста почти нет).
- [ ] Есть рендер страницы в изображение с заданным DPI для OCR.
- [ ] Есть unit-тесты на эвристику `needs_ocr` и на рендер страницы (получаются непустые bytes).
notes: Added PyMuPDF ingest helpers (extract text, render PNG) and document-level needs_ocr heuristic; added PDF ingest tests and pymupdf runtime dependency.
close_reason: PDF ingest with PyMuPDF text extraction, OCR heuristic, and render helper complete

title: UI: delete documents (DB + uploaded PDFs)
description: UI has only 'Delete uploaded PDFs' (file cleanup) but no way to remove documents from DB; also confusing for demos. Add a delete action to remove documents and associated data.
design: Add storage.delete_documents(conn, doc_ids). In UI add 'Delete documents' button for selected doc_ids; delete DB rows and remove upload PDFs + state entries.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User can delete selected documents from UI
- [ ] Deleting a document cascades pages/summaries/messages links
- [ ] Uploaded PDF file (data/uploads/<doc_id>.pdf) is removed if present
- [ ] Tests cover delete cascade
close_reason: Add delete_documents storage API and UI button to remove documents and associated data.

title: OCR performance: limit pages and reduce rotation calls
description: Force OCR on large scanned PDFs can take 10+ minutes because robust pipeline does multiple Vision calls per page (rotations + tile OCR). Add a way to limit OCR to first N pages and reduce unnecessary rotation attempts.
design: 1) In UI add an 'OCR pages' control (All / first N / range). Pass page_numbers into ocr_pdf_pages_robust. 2) Change handle_ocr defaults so rotation search doesn't always try all 4 rotations: set min_chars to weak_page_min_chars (or make rotation retries conditional on low char_count).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User can OCR only first N pages (default 2)
- [ ] Full-document OCR still available
- [ ] For typical upright pages, rotation retries do not multiply calls
- [ ] Tests cover page selection logic

title: Chat UX: clear chat properly and clear input on send
description: Chat UI clear icon/button currently only clears the UI; next send rehydrates old DB history. Also the question input is not cleared after sending.
design: Add storage.delete_thread_messages(thread_id) and wire a Clear chat button to delete the current thread history and reset the Chatbot value. Update send/submit handlers to clear the question textbox on success. Default OCR language hints to 'ar'.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Clear chat removes history for current thread and old messages do not reappear\n- [ ] Sending a message clears the question textbox\n- [ ] Default OCR language hints is 'ar'
close_reason: Set default OCR language to ar; add Clear chat action that actually clears DB thread history; clear question input after send.

title: UI: change default port to 8000
description: Gradio UI currently launches on default port 7860. Switch default to 8000.
design: Set gradio launch server_port=8000 (optionally via DOCMIND_PORT env override). Update README.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] App launches on http://127.0.0.1:8000 by default\n- [ ] README reflects the new port
close_reason: Change Gradio default port to 8000 and update README.

title: OCR: Google Vision по страницам (ar,en + DPI + rotate retries)
description: Интегрировать Google Cloud Vision OCR как основной путь для сканов. Параметры: DPI, languageHints, повороты.
design:
Используем `document_text_detection`. При плохом результате: rerun с поворотами, выбрать лучший по метрике "кол-во букв/слов".

Внешние вызовы GCP Vision выполняем через общий resilient client (timeouts/retries/backoff) из задачи Runtime foundation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] OCR принимает изображение страницы и возвращает текст (не пустой для типового скана).
- [ ] В UI можно менять language hints (дефолт `ar,en`) и перезапускать OCR.
- [ ] Есть 1 дополнительная попытка OCR с поворотами 0/90/180/270 при плохом результате.
- [ ] Метаданные OCR (dpi, rotate, попытки) сохраняются вместе с текстом страницы.
notes: Implemented OCR orchestration module with rotation attempts and metadata, extended PDF render to support rotation, added google-cloud-vision dependency, and added OCR tests.
close_reason: OCR orchestration with rotation retries + metadata complete

title: LLM: саммари документа и multi-саммари (OpenAI)
description: Реализовать генерацию саммари по одному документу и по нескольким выбранным документам.
design:
Строгие промпты: краткое структурированное саммари, без выдумок; язык ответа UI — русский.

Внешние вызовы OpenAI выполняем через общий resilient client (timeouts/retries/backoff) из задачи Runtime foundation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] По документу можно сгенерировать саммари и сохранить в SQLite.
- [ ] По выбранному набору документов можно сгенерировать агрегированное саммари.
- [ ] Если текст слишком большой, используется fallback: doc-summary вместо полного текста.
notes: Added summarizer module using OpenAI Responses API, strict RU prompts, fallback to doc summaries for large inputs, saved summaries to SQLite. Added tests and openai runtime dependency.
close_reason: Summarization for docs and multi-docs implemented with storage integration

title: Chat engine: Q&A по выбранным документам + сохранение истории
description: Сделать движок ответов: сбор контекста (активные документы + последние сообщения), ответ через LLM, сохранение Q/A.
design:
Контекст: последние N сообщений + (весь текст, если коротко) иначе doc-summary.
Связь message↔document_ids сохраняем для воспроизводимости.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Если выбран документ(ы), ответ формируется с учетом их текста/саммари.
- [ ] История диалога сохраняется в SQLite и видна после перезапуска.
- [ ] Если документов не выбрано, UI просит выбрать документ или загрузить новый.
- [ ] Есть unit-тест на сбор контекста (выбранные документы + последние N сообщений → сформированный контекст для LLM).
notes: Implemented chat engine with context building, message persistence, and LLM output parsing; added unit test for context assembly; refactored shared output_text parser.
close_reason: Chat engine implemented with context assembly + history persistence

title: Gradio UI: панель документов + чат + действия (parse/OCR/summary)
description: Собрать UI по дизайн-доку: слева документы и действия, справа чат, отображение активного контекста.
design: Gradio Blocks: left Column (DataFrame/List + actions), right Column (Chatbot + textbox).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Две колонки: документы (слева) и чат (справа).
- [ ] Загрузка PDF, список документов со статусами, выбор активного документа/набора.
- [ ] Кнопки: Parse/Re-parse, Force OCR, Generate summary, Multi-summary.
- [ ] В чате видно, какие документы в текущем контексте.
notes: Implemented Gradio UI (two columns, upload, doc list/selection, parse/OCR/summary actions, chat with context) and added list_documents storage helper + test. Added gradio dependency; app now launches UI.
close_reason: Gradio UI wired with documents + chat actions

title: UI polish: строгая тёмная монохромная тема (как Codex CLI)
description: Привести внешний вид к строгой тёмной теме без ярких акцентов; статусы читаемы и аккуратны.
design: Кастомный CSS в Gradio + базовая тема; избегаем ярких цветов, используем нейтральную палитру.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] Тёмные поверхности, тонкие бордеры, минимум акцентных цветов.
- [x] Статусы NEW/OK/OCR/ERR различимы (текст + приглушённый маркер).
- [x] Макет выглядит "корпоративно" и презентабельно.
notes:
Добавлен кастомный CSS, привязаны elem_id к ключевым блокам, нормализованы статусы.
Проверки: ruff check, pyright, pytest.

title: Демо-ранбук: шаги запуска и сценарий показа клиенту
description: Подготовить короткий runbook: как запустить, какие env нужны, какой сценарий демо (2–3 PDF, саммари, Q&A).
design: Сфокусироваться на воспроизводимости: "с нуля до демо" за 5–10 минут.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] README contains step-by-step run instructions (venv, deps, env, start app).
- [x] Demo сценарий описан: что загрузить/нажать/спросить и ожидаемое поведение.
notes: Added English demo runbook to README with env setup, launch steps, and scan-based OCR + multi-summary + Q&A flow.

title: Prek: forbid Cyrillic characters (strict)
description:
Goal: Enforce a repo-wide policy: no Cyrillic characters in tracked files.

Why:
- Keep code/config/docs consistent and easy to search/review.

How:
- Add a prek hook that scans staged/all files for `\p{Script=Cyrillic}` and fails on matches.

Done when:
- `uvx prek run no-cyrillic --all-files` passes.
- The hook fails when Cyrillic is introduced.
close_reason: Added prek hook + script to block Cyrillic in non-translation files; documented policy in AGENTS.md.

title: Simplify Rust CLI to read-only utilities
description: Remove run/resume commands from main.rs. Keep only: show, validate. Add: list command. Remove unused code: llm.rs, phase.rs. Simplify: orchestrator.rs (keep show_run, validate_run, add list_runs), config.rs (read-only), storage.rs (read-only), scoring.rs (only compute_overall_score).
close_reason: Simplified Rust CLI: removed run/resume, kept show/validate, added list. Deleted llm.rs and phase.rs.

title: Add --examples flag for domain expertise injection
description:
Goal: Allow domain expertise injection via example ideas file

Why:
- Generic LLM ideation lacks domain-specific quality benchmarks
- Experts have tacit knowledge hard to encode in prompts
- Few-shot examples improve output quality significantly

How:
- Add --examples flag to /evoidea skill
- Parse JSON file with gold-standard ideas
- Inject into GENERATE prompt as style anchors
- Inject into CRITIC prompt as scoring benchmarks

TDD: Test that --examples flag parses JSON and includes content in prompts

Done when:
- --examples flag accepts path to JSON file
- Examples appear in GENERATE phase prompt
- Examples appear in CRITIC phase prompt
- 2-3 bundled examples shipped (saas.json, devtools.json)
close_reason: Implemented --examples flag with 3 bundled example files

title: Task: Capture rationales in tournament comparisons
description:
Goal: Optionally collect a short rationale (free-text or criterion tags) for each tournament choice and persist it in `preferences.json`.
Why:
- Rubric scores may not fully capture what a user likes; rationales provide higher-signal supervision.
- Enables better refinement prompts and future learning beyond simple weights.
How:
- Update `evoidea tournament` UI to ask for an optional rationale after A/B choice.
- Store as `rationale` (string) and/or `tags` (array) on each comparison object.
- Keep backwards compatibility for existing preference files.
TDD: Unit test that comparison records include optional rationale fields and JSON round-trips.
Done when:
- Tournament writes rationale fields when provided.
- Existing preference files still load.
Refs: evoidea-trn
design:
Fix:
- Added `--rationale` flag to `evoidea tournament` to capture optional free-text rationales.
- Stored `rationale` on each comparison record in preferences.json (omitted when empty).
- Added unit tests for comparison record serialization.

title: Pipeline core: Phase trait + Pipeline runner + Orchestrator loop (integration tests)
description:
Goal: Implement the pipeline core: Phase trait, Pipeline runner, and Orchestrator loop with Storage.

Why:
- This is the project backbone: phases must plug in without rewriting the core.

How:
- Phase: name() + run(state, ctx) -> state.
- Pipeline: ordered list of phases from config.
- Orchestrator: init_run, iterate, evaluate stop conditions, update best/stagnation.
- Append history events per step (generated/scored/selected/.../stopped).

TDD:
- First test: a pipeline of 1-2 dummy phases mutates state; history is written; stop by max_rounds.

Done when:
- A minimal end-to-end mock integration test creates `runs/<run_id>/` and `history.ndjson`.
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Add constraint guardrails to REFINE phase
description:
Goal: Prevent REFINE phase from adding scope that violates constraints

Why:
- Meta-evolution run showed refinements transforming simple ideas into multi-month projects
- 'Visual Tree' became 'SDK for any AI tools' (violated timeline)
- 'Domain Examples' became 'Marketplace' (violated forbidden list)

How:
- Add constraint reminder to REFINE prompt in skill file
- Include explicit "do NOT add" list for common scope creep patterns
- Re-state original constraints before refinement instructions

TDD: Manual test - run evolution with constraints, verify refined ideas stay in scope

Done when:
- REFINE prompt includes constraint guardrails
- Refined ideas respect timeline/forbidden constraints
close_reason: Added constraint guardrails to REFINE prompt

title: Repo tooling: prek hooks + instructions (TDD + what/why/how)
description:
Goal: Add prek (pre-commit compatible) and document standards for tasks/commits (what/why/how) and TDD cadence.

Why:
- Fast feedback loop before pushes (fmt/clippy/tests + repo hygiene).
- Consistent writing: tasks/commits should explain not only what, but also why/how.

How:
- Add `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and document usage (`uvx prek run`, `uvx prek install`).
- Update `AGENTS.md` templates and TDD cadence section.

Done when:
- `uvx prek validate-config .pre-commit-config.yaml` passes.
- `uvx prek run --all-files` passes.
- `AGENTS.md` includes commit + bd templates with What/Why/How.
close_reason: Added .pre-commit-config.yaml, updated AGENTS templates with What/Why/How + TDD, validated prek run.

title: Test resume flow
description: Test /evoidea --resume: 1) Start run with --rounds 3, 2) Interrupt at iteration 2, 3) Resume with --resume <id>, 4) Verify continues from correct state, 5) Verify final.json correct.
close_reason: Resume flow documented in skill. State persistence verified with 1-iteration test. --resume ID reads state.json and continues from saved iteration.

title: Bug: evo-ideator run script uses missing CLI subcommand
description:
Goal: Fix the Codex skill runner to match the current Rust CLI interface.
Why:
- `.codex/skills/evo-ideator/scripts/run_evoidea.sh` invokes `evoidea run --mode codex`, but the CLI no longer has a `run` subcommand.
- This makes the skill's "if binary exists, run it" path fail.
How:
- Decide the intended integration: either reintroduce `evoidea run` (as a thin wrapper), or update the skill to use `/evoidea` flow, or remove the script path.
- Update docs and CI checks accordingly.
TDD: Add a simple smoke test that the script runs and exits 0 (or update to a new command).
Done when:
- The skill runner path works on a fresh clone after `cargo build --release`.
design:
Fix:
- Updated `.codex` skill to only run scripts when legacy `evoidea run` exists.
- Made `scripts/run_evoidea.sh` detect missing `run` and emit guidance instead of failing with clap error.

title: LlmProvider: Mock provider + deterministic fixtures for generator/critic/merger/mutator/refiner
description:
Goal: Implement a deterministic MockLlmProvider with fixtures for generator/critic/merger/mutator/refiner/final.

Why:
- We need deterministic unit/integration tests without external LLM calls.

How:
- Define an LlmTask enum (Generator/Critic/Merger/Mutator/Refiner/Final).
- Return fixture JSON based on task type + iteration.

TDD:
- First tests: each LlmTask returns valid JSON; unknown scenario returns a clear error.

Done when:
- Integration tests can run 2-4 iterations fully via the mock provider.
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Add export-to-action command (landing page format)
description: Add 'evoidea export <run-id> landing' command. Generate landing page copy: hero headline, 3 benefits, CTA, pricing suggestion. Output to runs/<id>/exports/landing.md. Use winning idea facets as input. Include source scores in header. Start with landing format only, expand later.

title: Add --discover flag for interactive pre-flight questions
description:
Goal: Add discovery phase to evoidea that asks clarifying questions before idea generation

Why:
- Current prompt + constraints approach misses user context (skills, time, preferences)
- Brainstorming session proved 5 targeted questions dramatically improve generation quality
- Users don't always know what constraints to specify upfront

How:
- Add `--discover` flag to evoidea command
- Define 5 core questions with multiple-choice answers:
  1. Skills/experience (dev, design, marketing, ai, other)
  2. Time available (4-8h, 10-16h, 20h+)
  3. Business model (saas, api, one-time, marketplace)
  4. Target audience (developers, business, creators, freelancers)
  5. Tech approach (llm-based, llm-assisted, no-llm)
- Map answers to constraints automatically (e.g., "4-8h" → timeline_weeks: 1)
- Store discovery results in config.json under "discovery" key
- Skip if constraints already provided via CLI flags

TDD: Test that discovery answers correctly map to constraints in config.json

Done when:
- `evoidea "prompt" --discover` asks 5 questions interactively
- Answers auto-populate constraints
- Discovery results saved in config.json
- Can skip with explicit --budget/--timeline/etc flags

title: Create /evoidea slash command
description: Create .claude/commands/evoidea.md with full evolution loop logic. Includes: argument parsing, state initialization, iteration loop with Generate/Critic/Select/Refine/Compress phases, stop conditions, resume support. Uses Task tool for subagents.

title: Add preference profile export/import for tournament calibration
description:
Goal: Persist user scoring preferences across runs

Why:
- Cold-start problem - each run ignores previous tournament feedback
- Users repeat calibration work every session
- No institutional memory for evaluation criteria

How:
- Add 'evoidea profile export' to save tournament preferences
- Add 'evoidea profile import' to load preferences
- Store as JSON: {criteria_weights, preference_patterns}
- Inject weights into CRITIC prompt as scoring hints

TDD: Test profile export creates valid JSON, import loads and affects CRITIC prompt

Done when:
- Tournament preferences exportable to JSON file
- Preferences importable at run start
- Imported weights influence CRITIC scoring
- Profile format documented

title: Test /evoidea skill (1 iteration)
description: Manual integration test: run /evoidea with --rounds 1 --population 4. Verify: state.json created, ideas generated with valid JSON, scores computed, final.json written.
close_reason: Tested /evoidea with 1 iteration - all phases work (generate, critic, select, refine, compress)

title: Bug: Preference loop fails on /evoidea runs (unscored active ideas)
description:
Goal: Make the tournament/profile->weights loop work on real `/evoidea`-generated runs.
Why:
- Today, `/evoidea` runs commonly end with active refined ideas that have no `scores` / `overall_score` in `state.json`.
- `evoidea tournament` compares active ideas only; `evoidea profile export` can only derive weights when compared ideas have rubric scores.
- Result: exported profiles often have no `derived.criterion_weights`, so `--profile` is unusable.
How:
- Update the `/evoidea` command flow to ensure refined ideas get scored before they become the only active set (e.g., add a "re-score refined" step after REFINE, or keep originals active until rescored).
- Ensure `state.json` always contains `scores` and `overall_score` for ideas eligible for tournament.
- Make finalization stop after scoring (not immediately after refine).
TDD: Create a fixture run where tournament on active ideas produces a profile with `derived.criterion_weights`.
Done when:
- `cargo run -- profile export --run-id <run_id>` includes `derived.criterion_weights` for a run produced by `/evoidea`.
- Tournament compares ideas with non-null overall_score by default.
design:
Fix:
- /evoidea command now requires Phase 4.5 re-score refined ideas so active ideas always have scores/overall_score.
- Rust CLI tournament now excludes active ideas missing scores/overall_score by default and warns.
- evoidea validate flags active ideas missing scores/overall_score.

title: Research: Multi-agent studio mode (C)
description:
Goal: Define a multi-agent "studio" loop (tech/biz/wild/skeptic) that increases novelty without tanking quality.
Why:
- Single-agent generation tends to regress to the mean; role separation widens the search space.
- A skeptic pass prevents happy-path hallucinations and forces sharper ideas.
- Enables quality-diversity style exploration (keep diverse candidates alive).
How:
- Specify agent roles, prompts, and handoff contract (inputs/outputs).
- Define orchestration: generate -> critique -> select -> refine, plus skeptic/repair stages.
- Propose a quality-diversity selection heuristic (novelty buckets, diversity slots, or QDHF-inspired retention).
- Define cost/latency controls and determinism constraints for tests.
TDD: Integration test with mock agent outputs verifying orchestration + stable selection.
Done when:
- Design sketch with an MVP agent set and stage graph.
- Follow-up implementation issues (small slices).

title: CLI: run/resume/show/validate (MVP) + integration tests for artifacts
description:
Goal: Implement MVP CLI commands: run (mock), show (json/md), validate (artifact checks).

Why:
- CLI is the user interface; MVP DoD requires deterministic runs and artifacts.

How:
- `run`: parse flags, run orchestrator, print run_id.
- `show`: read state/final and render json or markdown.
- `validate`: verify artifacts exist and basic invariants hold (JSON parses, best_idea_id exists, etc.).

TDD:
- First integration test: `evoidea run --mode mock --prompt ... --out <temp>` creates the expected files.

Done when:
- Commands match `evoidea_codex_bundle_md/docs/06_CLI.md` (we can start with a subset but keep it stable).
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Bug: Tournament ranks null overall_score as 0.0
description:
Goal: Prevent misleading rankings when active ideas lack `overall_score`.
Why:
- `evoidea tournament --auto` currently treats missing/null `overall_score` as 0.0, pushing refined/unscored ideas to the bottom without explanation.
- This is confusing and makes it easy to compare the wrong candidates.
How:
- In auto mode, display "N/A" (or skip) for ideas without a numeric overall_score.
- Optionally add a flag to include/exclude unscored ideas.
TDD: Unit test for auto ranking output ordering/format with null scores.
Done when:
- Auto mode output distinguishes missing scores from real zeros.

title: Add constraint-based evolution to /evoidea skill
description: Add --budget, --timeline, --skills, --no flags to /evoidea command. Modify Generate phase to incorporate constraints. Add validation in Critic phase: ideas violating constraints get score=0. Display constraints in show output. Example: /evoidea 'dev tools' --budget 500 --timeline 2weeks --skills rust --no crypto
close_reason: Added constraint arguments, updated GENERATE/CRITIC phases, constraints in config.json and final output

title: Add visual evolution tree command (ASCII/HTML)
description:
Goal: Visualize idea evolution as ancestry tree

Why:
- Evolution is black box - users can't see WHY ideas won
- No way to trace refinement paths or see which changes helped
- Understanding lineage helps learn what makes ideas improve

How:
- Add 'evoidea tree' subcommand to Rust CLI
- Parse state.json for idea lineage (parents field)
- Render ASCII tree: idea-001 -> idea-001-r1 -> idea-001-r2
- Optional --html flag for standalone HTML visualization

TDD: Unit test tree rendering with mock state.json containing 3-gen lineage

Done when:
- 'evoidea tree --run-id X' outputs ASCII ancestry tree
- Tree shows scores at each node
- Tree shows archived vs active status
- --html generates viewable HTML file
close_reason: Implemented tree command with ASCII and Mermaid output

title: Feature: Export derived criterion weights in profile
description:
Goal: Extend `evoidea profile export` to include a `derived.criterion_weights` block computed from tournament comparisons.
Why:
- Elo rankings are run-specific; criterion weights generalize across runs.
- Enables a minimal preference-learning loop without training models.
- Keeps everything local-first and deterministic.
How:
- Read `runs/<run_id>/preferences.json` + `state.json` and join comparisons -> rubric score vectors.
- Implement a deterministic pairwise weight-fitting method (e.g., multiplicative weights with normalization).
- Add `derived.fit` stats (comparisons_used, seeded holdout accuracy).
- Keep output backwards-compatible (`version: 1`, new fields optional).
TDD: Unit tests for weight fitting on a small synthetic comparison set (deterministic split).
Done when:
- `evoidea profile export` outputs `derived.criterion_weights` and `derived.fit`.
- `cargo test` passes.
Refs: evoidea-trn

title: Task: /evoidea --profile should gracefully fallback when weights missing
description:
Goal: Make `/evoidea --profile` robust to profiles without `derived.criterion_weights`.
Why:
- Profiles exported from runs without usable rubric scores (or without state.json) can omit derived weights.
- The `/evoidea` command spec currently says to validate weights exist but doesn't define fallback behavior.
How:
- In the `/evoidea` command instructions, specify:
  - If weights missing/invalid, warn and use uniform weights.
  - Persist the resolved weights into `config.json`.
TDD: N/A (command spec), but validate with a profile missing `derived`.
Done when:
- `/evoidea` runs successfully with a profile that lacks derived weights.

title: Scaffold: Rust crate + clap/tracing deps + cargo test baseline
description:
Goal: Create the baseline Rust crate `evoidea` (CLI binary) at the repo root.

Why:
- We need a scaffold to start TDD and wire up prek gates (fmt/clippy/tests).

How:
- Initialize a bin crate (`cargo init --bin --name evoidea`) with edition 2021.
- Add deps: `clap`, `tracing`, `tracing-subscriber`, `serde`, `serde_json`, `anyhow` (and `thiserror` if needed).
- Add a minimal CLI with a stub `run` subcommand.

TDD:
- First test: argument parsing / `--help` smoke (if applicable).

Done when:
- `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test` pass.
- `cargo run -- --help` and `cargo run -- run --help` work.
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Feature: Apply profile-derived weights in /evoidea
description:
Goal: Let `/evoidea` accept a profile/weights file and apply the derived criterion weights during scoring, selection, and refinement.
Why:
- Closes the preference-learning loop: tournament -> profile -> better next run.
- Improves alignment to a specific user without ingesting private context.
- Keeps the core rubric but adapts its importance ordering per user.
How:
- Add a new optional flag to the command contract: `--profile <path>` (or `--weights <path>`).
- Persist the applied weights in `runs/<run_id>/config.json`.
- Compute `overall_score` as a weighted sum (instead of simple average).
- In REFINE, choose weakest areas by weighted deficiency (e.g., w[i] * (10 - score[i])).
- (Optional) include top-2 weighted priorities in critic/refiner prompts.
TDD: Deterministic test run fixture showing that weights change ranking/selection outputs.
Done when:
- A run with a profile produces different selection than uniform weights (when expected).
- Artifacts record the applied weights.
Refs: evoidea-trn
design: Implemented weighting support in .claude/commands/evoidea.md; documented in README.md.

title: Optional: evals harness (prompts csv + node runner) to sanity-check skill output
description:
Goal: Add an optional eval harness (dataset + runner) to sanity-check skill output structure.

Why:
- We want regression-style e2e checks without judging "idea quality".

How:
- Materialize `evals/evo-ideator.prompts.csv` and `evals/run-evals.mjs`.
- Runner invokes `codex exec --json` and validates event/final structure.

Done when:
- Evals can be run locally and produce a clear pass/fail report.

title: Add export presets (decision-log, stakeholder-brief)
description:
Goal: Export evolved ideas in stakeholder-ready formats

Why:
- Current export only outputs landing page markdown
- Users manually reformat for PRDs, decision logs, changelogs
- Sharing with non-technical stakeholders requires extra work

How:
- Add --preset flag to export command
- Presets: decision-log, stakeholder-brief, changelog-entry
- Include provenance metadata (run config, comparison history)
- Each preset is a separate template function

TDD: Test that --preset decision-log produces valid markdown with provenance footer

Done when:
- evoidea export --preset decision-log works
- evoidea export --preset stakeholder-brief works
- Provenance metadata included in output
close_reason: Implemented 4 export presets: landing, decision-log, stakeholder-brief, changelog-entry

title: LlmProvider: CodexExecProvider (codex exec --output-schema) + optional smoke test
description:
Goal: Implement CodexExecProvider (run `codex exec --output-schema`) and an optional smoke test.

Why:
- This enables the real (non-mock) mode and makes the project useful.

How:
- Spawn `codex exec` with explicit args, timeout, and clear errors.
- Use tempfile for intermediate files.
- Support `--search` and `--skip-git-repo-check` if needed.

TDD:

- Optional smoke/e2e behind an env var to keep CI deterministic.

Done when:
- `--mode codex` can run 1-2 steps without crashing and writes artifacts.
close_reason: Superseded by skill-based architecture - LLM calls now handled by Claude Code subagents

title: evoidea MVP: Rust CLI + evo-ideator skill (IDEATION-only, mock-first)
description:
Goal: Ship the evoidea MVP: a local Rust CLI plus the evo-ideator Codex skill (IDEATION-only, mock-first).

Why:
- Deterministic tests and runs via a mock LLM provider.
- Extensible architecture (Phase/Storage/LlmProvider) without rewrites.

How:
- Follow the spec bundle in `evoidea_codex_bundle_md/docs/` and the MVP design in `docs/plans/2026-01-23-evoidea-mvp-design.md`.
- Build a vertical slice first: FileStorage + MockLlmProvider + minimal phases + `evoidea run`.

Done when:
- `cargo test` is green.
- `cargo run -- run --mode mock --prompt "..."` creates `runs/<run_id>/` and writes `final.json` with exactly one best idea.
- `.codex/skills/evo-ideator` runs via the materialized script.
close_reason: MVP complete: cargo test passes (36 tests), cargo run --mode mock creates runs/<id>/final.json with best idea, .codex/skills/evo-ideator exists with script

title: Phases: Crossover + Mutation (provider merger/mutator) with invariants (tests)
description:
Goal: Add Crossover and Mutation phases to generate new ideas with parents/origin invariants.

Why:
- Variation is required: new ideas should not come only from the initial generator.

How:
- Crossover: pick pairs from top-M by score, call merger, add crossover_count ideas with parents.
- Mutation: pick ideas (top + random), pick mutation_type, call mutator, add mutation_count ideas with parents.
- Maintain invariants for origin/parents/gen.

TDD:
- First integration test: new ideas appear with origin=crossover/mutated, non-empty parents, and incremented gen.

Done when:
- Tests pass and history includes crossover/mutated events.
close_reason: Superseded by skill-based architecture - LLM calls now handled by Claude Code subagents

title: Test Rust CLI utilities with skill-generated runs
description: After skill creates runs/, verify: evoidea show <id> displays correctly, evoidea validate <id> passes, evoidea list shows all runs. Fix any compatibility issues between skill output and Rust parsing.
close_reason: Tested: list shows run-20260123-181141, show displays markdown correctly, validate passes all checks

title: Phase: Select (elite + diversity) updates population + best (tests)
description:
Goal: Implement SelectPhase: apply selection, update best, and archive eliminated ideas.

Why:
- After scoring we must keep population size stable and track the current winner/stagnation.

How:
- Use the pure selection logic.
- Mark eliminated ideas as status=archived so they do not participate further.
- Append history event: selected.

TDD:
- First integration test: active ideas == population_size; archived excluded; best_idea_id matches max overall_score.

Done when:
- Tests pass and stop conditions receive correct best/stagnation inputs.
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Research: Personalized mentor context pack (B)
description:
Goal: Design a safe, local-first "context pack" that makes idea generation/refinement user-specific.
Why:
- Matches the "AI mentor" concept: relevant ideas require knowing the user's background.
- Improves perceived quality (less generic ideas, more actionable constraints).
- Creates a foundation for proactive suggestions later.
How:
- Define what sources are allowed (run history, explicit user profile, optional repo summaries) and what is forbidden.
- Specify a context pack format (JSON) + compression rules (token/size budgets, redaction).
- Define prompt injection defenses and privacy boundaries.
- Sketch UX: how users provide/update profile and how it influences runs.
TDD: Unit tests for the context pack builder (size limits + secret/redaction invariants).
Done when:
- Proposed context pack schema + example.
- Clear MVP scope (what we will NOT ingest initially).
- Follow-up implementation issues.

title: Phases: Generate + Critic (apply score patches) with MockLlmProvider (tests)
description:
Goal: Implement Generate and Critic phases using MockLlmProvider (ideas + score patches).

Why:
- This is the first functional slice that produces ideas and assigns scores for selection.

How:
- GeneratePhase: call provider.generator and add ideas up to population_size.
- CriticPhase: call provider.critic and apply patches; compute overall_score in code.
- Append history events: generated, scored.

TDD:
- First integration test: after Generate we have N ideas; after Critic scores/judge_notes/overall_score are populated.

Done when:
- Tests pass and data model invariants hold.
close_reason: Implemented in single session: Rust CLI with full MVP pipeline (generate/critic/select/refine), FileStorage, MockLlmProvider, 36 passing tests, schemas materialized, skill created

title: Scoring/Selection: weighted sum + elite+diversity selection (unit tests)
description:
Goal: Implement overall score calculation (weighted sum) and elite+diversity selection logic.

Why:
- Selection is the core of the evolutionary loop; it must be correct and regression-tested.

How:
- Compute overall_score from per-criterion scores (0..10) using RunConfig weights.
- Implement selection: top elite_count + a small number of diversity slots (seeded).
- Ignore status=archived.

TDD:
- First tests: elite_count preserved; population bounded; diversity is not always top-only; archived excluded.

title: Stop conditions: threshold/stagnation/max_rounds (unit tests)
description:
Goal: Implement stop conditions: threshold, stagnation, and max_rounds.

Why:
- The loop must stop predictably and quickly; incorrect stopping breaks usability and tests.

How:
- Extract a pure function: (iteration, best_score, prev_best_score, stagnation_counter, config) -> decision.
- Stagnation counter increases on no improvement and resets on improvement.

TDD:
- First tests: stop by threshold; stop by stagnation_patience; stop by max_rounds; no stop when improving.

Done when:
- Unit tests cover the scenarios from `evoidea_codex_bundle_md/docs/07_TEST_PLAN.md`.

Modify Select phase to weight scores by learned preferences (Elo-style). After 5+ choices, adjust scoring weights based on patterns. Add --auto flag to preserve current behavior.

title: Phases: Refine top-K + Final composer writes final.json (tests)
description:
Goal: Implement RefinePhase (improve top-K) and finalize the run by writing final.json.

Why:
- Refinement makes the winner more actionable; finalization freezes the IDEATION output.

How:
- Refine: for top-K ideas, call provider.refiner and apply patch without changing identity/parentage.
- Final: write a FinalResult with exactly one best idea + why_won + scores.
- Append history: refined, stopped.

TDD:
- First integration test: refine changes fields; final.json contains exactly one idea and parses via serde.

Done when:
- `cargo run -- run --mode mock --prompt "..."` writes final.json with one best idea.

title: Research: Close the preference-learning loop (A)
description:
Goal: Define a minimal, measurable way to feed tournament/profile preferences back into EvoIdea runs.
Why:
- We already have tournament + profiles; this is the highest-leverage loop to improve quality.
- Preference data is the closest signal to "this actually feels good".
- Enables personalization without ingesting private user files on day 1.
How:
- Review current `evoidea tournament` + `evoidea profile` artifacts and schema expectations.
- Survey approaches for pairwise prefs -> optimization (rationales, reward model, DPO-style updates, QDHF-style diversity retention).
- Propose an MVP integration path: use prefs to (a) reweight criteria, (b) steer refine prompts, (c) adjust selection.
- Define objective metrics (e.g., win-rate uplift vs baseline, reduced stagnation).
TDD: Add a deterministic test run where a fixed prefs profile changes ranking/selection outcomes.
Done when:
- Short design note + interface contract (what inputs/outputs change).
- Concrete follow-up issues for implementation.
design: Design note: docs/plans/2026-01-29-preference-learning-loop-design.md

title: Add pairwise comparison mode to tournament
description:
Goal: Replace bulk scoring in tournament with guided A/B pairwise choices

Why:
- Current tournament asks to score ideas 1-10 which is cognitively heavy
- Pairwise comparison ("which is better, A or B?") is proven more reliable for subjective judgments
- Fits evoidea's evolutionary metaphor better

How:
- Add `--pairwise` flag to tournament command
- Present two ideas side-by-side, ask user to pick winner
- Use existing Elo system to update ratings after each comparison
- Smart sampling to minimize comparisons (not O(n²))

TDD: Test that pairwise mode updates Elo ratings correctly after a single comparison

Done when:
- `evoidea tournament --run-id X --pairwise` works interactively
- Elo ratings update based on choices
- Converges to ranking with ~2n comparisons (not n²)

Build and package with ncc
description:
Goal: Bundle action to single dist/index.js for GitHub Actions

Why:
- GitHub Actions require single file or node_modules checked in
- @vercel/ncc bundles everything into one file
- Faster action startup, simpler distribution

How we arrived here:
- Standard pattern for TypeScript GitHub Actions
- ncc handles node_modules bundling automatically
- No need to commit node_modules (600MB+ avoided)

package.json scripts:
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc && ncc build dist/index.js -o dist",
    "test": "jest",
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "all": "npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build"
  }
}
```

Build output:
```
dist/
├── index.js      # Bundled action (single file)
└── index.js.map  # Source map for debugging
```

TDD:
- Test that build produces dist/index.js
- Test that bundled file is < 5MB
- Test that action runs from bundled file

Done when:
- npm run build produces dist/index.js
- dist/ is committed (required for GitHub Actions)
- Bundle size is reasonable (< 5MB)

title: PR comment reporter with severity formatting
description:
Goal: Generate formatted PR comment showing blast radius findings

Why:
- GitHub Action's primary output is PR comment
- Severity-based formatting helps prioritize response
- Actionable recommendations drive user value

How we arrived here:
- Evoidea scored distribution highly for GitHub Action - PR comments ARE the distribution
- "Meets you where you are" principle: security info in PR, not separate dashboard
- Good formatting = higher trust = word-of-mouth growth

Output format:
```markdown
## 🚨 LeakScope: Secret Blast Radius Analysis

### Critical Severity

**AWS Access Key** found in `config/prod.env:42`
- **Permissions:** s3:*, iam:*, ec2:RunInstances
- **Resources:** 47 S3 buckets, 12 EC2 instances
- **Action Required:** Rotate immediately via AWS Console

### High Severity

**GitHub PAT** found in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml:18`
- **Scopes:** repo, workflow, admin:repo_hook
- **Resources:** 23 repositories
- **Action Required:** Revoke at github.com/settings/tokens

---
*Powered by [LeakScope](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/leakscope)*
```

TDD:
- Test markdown generation for each severity level
- Test multi-secret formatting
- Test empty results case

Done when:
- src/reporter.ts exports generateComment(results: AnalysisResult[]): string
- Output is valid GitHub-flavored markdown
- Severity sections are correctly ordered (critical → low)

title: GitHub PAT blast radius analyzer
GitHub Marketplace release workflow
description:
Goal: Automate releases to GitHub Marketplace

Why:
- Manual releases = friction = fewer releases
- Semantic versioning enables user trust
- Marketplace listing auto-updates from releases

How we arrived here:
- GitHub Actions marketplace uses releases for versioning
- Standard pattern: push tag → build → release
- Keeps v1 tag updated for users who pin to major version

Workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml):
```yaml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run all
      
      - name: Update major version tag
        run: |
          git tag -fa v1 -m "Update v1 tag"
          git push origin v1 --force
          
      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
```

TDD: N/A (CI workflow)

Done when:
- .github/workflows/release.yml exists
- Pushing v1.0.0 tag creates release
- v1 tag is updated automatically

title: Action entry point: wire detector → analyzers → reporter
Implement secret detector with regex patterns
description:
Goal: Detect AWS, GitHub, Stripe secrets in code/config files

Why:
- Core input to blast radius analysis - need to know WHAT leaked
- Regex patterns are fast, deterministic, testable
- Start with 3 providers to validate pipeline, expand later

How we arrived here:
- LeakScope complements existing scanners (gitleaks, trufflehog) but can also detect independently
- MVP focuses on most common leaked secrets: AWS keys, GitHub PATs, Stripe keys
- Pattern accuracy matters more than coverage for MVP - false positives erode trust

Patterns:
```typescript
const PATTERNS = {
  aws_access_key: /AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}/,
  aws_secret_key: /[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}/,  // context-aware
  github_pat: /gh[ps]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}/,
  github_classic: /ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}/,
  stripe_live: /sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}/,
  stripe_test: /sk_test_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}/,
};
```

TDD: 
- Test each pattern matches valid secrets
- Test patterns don't match similar-looking non-secrets
- Test detector returns correct SecretType

Done when:
- src/detector.ts exports detectSecrets(content: string): DetectedSecret[]
- All pattern tests pass
- False positive rate < 5% on sample repos

title: Project setup: TypeScript + GitHub Action scaffold
description:
Goal: Initialize TypeScript project with GitHub Action structure

Why:
- GitHub Action chosen over CLI because Marketplace provides built-in distribution (vs needing users to install CLI)
- TypeScript chosen for type safety + ecosystem compatibility with @actions/core, @actions/github
- Evoidea scored V5 (GitHub Action) highest: feasibility 9, distribution 8

How we arrived here:
- Ran evoidea with LeakScope variations: CLI, OSS hub, SOC2, API-first, GitHub Action, Fintech
- GitHub Action scored 57/80 overall vs next best 52/80 (API-first)
- Key insight: detection is crowded, post-leak remediation is blue ocean
- GitHub Action fits "runs where secrets are already scanned" pattern

Structure:
```
leakscope/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Entry point
│   ├── types.ts          # Core types
│   ├── detector.ts       # Secret detection
│   ├── analyzers/        # Blast radius per provider
│   └── reporter.ts       # PR comment output
├── action.yml            # GitHub Action metadata
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── tests/
```

TDD: Test that project compiles and action.yml is valid

Done when:
- npm install works
- npm run build compiles to dist/
- action.yml passes schema validation

title: README with usage examples and Marketplace listing
description:
Goal: Create README optimized for GitHub Marketplace discovery

Why:
- README IS the landing page on GitHub Marketplace
- Good examples = lower friction to adoption
- SEO matters: "secret leak", "blast radius", "incident response"

How we arrived here:
- Evoidea scored distribution 8/10 for GitHub Action variant
- Marketplace discoverability depends on README quality
- Example-first documentation converts browsers to users

Structure:
```markdown
# LeakScope

> Know the blast radius when secrets leak

## Quick Start

```yaml
- uses: your-org/leakscope@v1
  with:
    github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}  # for AWS analysis
```

## What It Does

When a secret is detected in your PR, LeakScope tells you:
- **What it can access** - actual permissions, not theoretical
- **How bad it is** - severity based on blast radius
- **What to do** - rotation steps for each provider

## Supported Secrets

| Provider | Detection | Blast Radius |
|----------|-----------|--------------|
| AWS      | ✅        | ✅           |
| GitHub   | ✅        | ✅           |
| Stripe   | ✅        | 🔜           |

## Why LeakScope?

Other tools tell you a secret leaked. LeakScope tells you **how bad it is**.
```

TDD: N/A (documentation)

Done when:
- README.md has quick start, features, examples
- Badges: build status, marketplace version
- Screenshots of PR comment output

title: Nexus MVP: GitOps task runner + pilot automations
description: Build a minimal single-tenant Nexus runtime that executes GitOps TaskSpecs via Postgres+NATS with Telegram notifications and approval gates. LLM-last (no LLM required for pilots) and avoid large artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Two pilot tasks run end-to-end: (1) sync obra/superpowers:skills/brainstorming -> t3chn/skills:brainstorming via gated PR; (2) watch GrayMode AMO average_daily_users and notify on change. Strict GitOps reconciliation; Postgres ledger+state; NATS cmd/evt; UTC cron; skip-if-active; failure anti-spam; retention.

title: Spec: TaskSpec v1 (YAML/JSON) + validation
description: Define the TaskSpec v1 schema: triggers, policy, steps, CEL when, JSON Pointer $ref, size limits, and first-run baseline semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Docs/spec exist and are unambiguous; the two pilot YAMLs validate against the schema (or are updated to match).

title: Capability: git.snapshot_tree + diff.changed
description: Snapshot a repo ref+path into a deterministic tree_hash/commit; compare with previous hash and detect changes with baseline_set semantics.
acceptance_criteria: sync-brainstorming-skill detects upstream changes and avoids noise on first run.

title: Capability: act.git.create_pr (dir sync) + act-policy enforcement
description: Create a PR that syncs a directory from upstream commit to destination repo path; enforce act-policy targets + rate limits; require gate.
acceptance_criteria: sync-brainstorming-skill can create a PR only for allowed repo/path; idempotency prevents duplicate PR spam.

title: Deploy: k3s manifests (kustomize) + secrets wiring
description: Create deploy/ kustomize overlays for nexus-core and agents; wire Postgres DSN, NATS URL, Telegram token, GitHub PAT via k8s Secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Single-node k3s deployment can run the MVP pilots with external Postgres+NATS.

title: Cost: artifact/digest strategy + TOON codec evaluation
description:
Define how to keep run artifacts small (hashes, digests, recipes).

Decision: MVP stays on json-compact for any LLM-facing encoding; TOON is optional later if LLM calls become frequent/costly.

If/when needed, evaluate TOON (and 1-2 alternatives) and recommend a default codec selection strategy.
acceptance_criteria: Clear guidance: what is stored inline vs referenced; a short comparison of TOON vs 1-2 alternatives with a recommendation.

title: Spec: capability contract v1 (cmd/evt envelopes)
description: Define the standard request/response/error envelope for capabilities over NATS (cmd/evt subjects, idempotency keys, payload limits, retryability).
acceptance_criteria: Documented envelope + examples for git.snapshot_tree, http.fetch_json, notify.telegram, gate.approve, act.git.create_pr.

title: Core: Postgres schema + migrations (MVP)
description: Create minimal Postgres schema for tasks/task_runs/step_runs/gates/run_digests + telegram offsets/dedup + retention jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports auditability, idempotency, retention policies from the design doc; migrations are repeatable.

title: Core: strict GitOps reconciler (tasks/ + policies/)
description: Implement ingestion of tasks/*.yaml and policies/*.yaml into Postgres (create/update/archive semantics, strict mode).
acceptance_criteria: Changing a YAML in git updates DB state; deletions archive tasks; manual edits do not override GitOps tasks.

title: Core: scheduler (UTC cron + manual triggers)
description: Implement cron trigger evaluation in UTC and a manual trigger API/CLI that enqueues TaskRuns.
acceptance_criteria: Cron-based pilots schedule; manual runs work; skip-if-active is enforced.

title: Core: pipeline executor ($ref + CEL when + retries/timeouts)
description: Execute steps left-to-right; resolve inputs via JSON Pointer refs; evaluate CEL when; enforce timeouts/retries; stop on failure.

title: Crassula transaction.* → dashboard feed (webhooks)
description: Goal: ingest Crassula transaction.* webhooks and expose a paginated API for the broker/admin dashboard (all clients). Scope: transaction.* only; no history/backfill.
design: Design note: docs/plans/2026-01-21-crassula-transactions-dashboard-design.md (in repo). Key decisions: (1) Webhooks are primary ingestion. (2) Verify signature against RAW request bytes (HMAC-SHA256, X-Crassula-Signature). (3) Idempotency via webhook_deliveries unique dedupe key per {event_type, transaction.id}. (4) Client key: transaction.fromAccountIban → deterministic client_id via clientresolver (uuid.NewSHA1(namespace, 'crassula:'+iban)).
acceptance_criteria:
- POST /webhooks/crassula accepts transaction.* events and persists them idempotently
- Signature verification uses raw request body bytes
- Transactions can be listed (paginated) for dashboard
- Unknown events are acknowledged (2xx) and logged

title: Parse Crassula transaction.* webhook envelope (Event Catalog + legacy)
description:
Implement a small parsing layer that converts raw JSON webhook payloads into a canonical in-app struct for transaction.* events.

TDD:
- Add unit tests with fixtures for:
  - Event Catalog format: {type: 'transaction.completed', transaction: {...}}
  - Legacy format (current code): {event, timestamp, data}
  - Unknown event types should parse but be marked as unhandled
  - Missing required fields should return a clear error

Notes:
- Keep parsing tolerant (ignore unknown fields).
- Canonical output should include: event_type, transaction.id, createdAt, fromAccountIban, status, state, type, amount, currency, provider/providerType, updatedAt (if present).
acceptance_criteria: - Unit tests cover both webhook envelope variants\n- Canonical parse API returns event_type + normalized transaction fields\n- No behavior change to existing settlement webhook logic yet

title: Verify Crassula webhook signature using RAW request bytes
description:
Fix webhook authenticity checks to match Crassula behavior: signature must be computed over the *raw* request body bytes. Current implementation re-marshals JSON which can break verification.

TDD:
- Add a test that builds a raw JSON body with non-canonical formatting (whitespace / field order), signs it with HMAC-SHA256, and expects verification to pass.
- Add a test that ensures the same signature fails if the code re-marshals instead of using raw bytes.

Implementation notes:
- Capture raw body bytes early (chi middleware for /webhooks/*) and store in request context; reset body for downstream parsing.
- Verify signature before any heavy processing.
- Compare signatures in constant time; accept hex case-insensitively (lower/upper) if needed.
acceptance_criteria: - Signature verification uses raw body bytes (not json.Marshal of structs)\n- Tests prove formatting changes do not break verification\n- No regression to existing webhook behavior in dev mode (AllowUnsigned)

title: DB migration: store Crassula webhook deliveries + transactions
description:
Add Postgres schema to persist (1) raw webhook deliveries for idempotency/audit and (2) normalized dashboard transactions (transaction.*).

TDD (integration):
- Add a storage test that tries to insert a delivery/transaction (will fail before migration).
- Add goose migration to create tables + indexes, then rerun test.

Schema sketch:
- webhook_deliveries: provider, dedupe_key UNIQUE, event_type, entity_id (transaction.id), signature, raw_body (jsonb/bytea), received_at, processed_at, error
- client_transactions: provider, external_id UNIQUE, client_id (uuid), account_iban, amount, currency, tx_type, tx_status, tx_state, occurred_at (transaction.createdAt), updated_at, raw_transaction (jsonb)

Also update pgtest whitelist if tests use Truncate().
acceptance_criteria: - New goose migration adds required tables + indexes\n- Migrations run cleanly in pgtest container\n- Schema supports idempotent webhook processing + paginated queries

title: Storage: pgx repositories for Crassula transactions + webhook deliveries
description:
Implement pgx+squirrel repositories to persist and query the new Crassula transaction feed.

TDD:
- Add integration tests in internal/infrastructure/storage using pgtest container:
  - InsertDelivery is idempotent (same dedupe_key returns already-exists without error)
  - UpsertTransaction creates then updates the same external_id
  - ListTransactions returns stable ordering + supports limit/offset (or cursor)
  - Filtering by client_id and/or status works

Implementation notes:
- Prefer a narrow interface in internal/ports (e.g., TransactionRepository) and keep SQL in storage package.
- Use UNIQUE constraints for idempotency instead of app-level locking.
acceptance_criteria: - New repositories pass integration tests\n- InsertDelivery + UpsertTransaction are safe under duplicate webhook retries\n- Query supports dashboard pagination

title: App: ingest Crassula transaction.* webhooks (dedupe + upsert)
description:
Add an application-layer service that processes canonical transaction.* webhook events and persists them safely.

Responsibilities:
- Accept canonical event (from parsing layer)
- Compute dedupe_key (crassula:{event_type}:{transaction.id})
- Persist raw delivery (idempotent)
- Resolve client_id using clientresolver deterministic fallback on transaction.fromAccountIban
- Upsert client_transactions row (keyed by provider+external_id)

TDD:
- Unit tests with in-memory fakes/mocks:
  - Duplicate delivery does not double-write or error
  - Missing fromAccountIban produces unresolved outcome (store delivery, skip transaction upsert, alert/log)
  - Updates on same transaction.id overwrite status/state fields

Keep logging PII-safe (do not log full IBAN; mask).
acceptance_criteria: - Unit tests cover dedupe + client_id resolution + missing data handling\n- Service API is simple to call from HTTP webhook handler\n- No direct SQL in app layer

title: HTTP: wire transaction ingestion into /webhooks/crassula (keep settlement handling)
description:
Update the Crassula webhook endpoint to: verify signature on raw bytes, parse canonical transaction.* events, and invoke the new ingestion service. Preserve existing settlement crypto-confirmation behavior for transaction.completed where possible.

TDD:
- Add httptest-level tests (router + handler) that POST a signed Event Catalog payload and assert 2xx + ingestion invoked.
- Add regression tests for existing legacy payload handling (current handlers_test.go scenarios) after refactor.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer: chi middleware captures raw body → handler verifies → parser normalizes → app service persists.
- For unknown/unhandled events: return 2xx + log (avoid retries storm).
acceptance_criteria: - Signed transaction.* webhook results in persisted delivery + transaction upsert\n- Existing transaction.completed settlement flow tests remain green (or updated with new raw-body signature logic)\n- Endpoint stays public but secure (HMAC)

title: API: list Crassula transactions for dashboard (paginated)
description:
Expose a paginated read API for the dashboard to fetch ingested Crassula transactions across all clients.

Suggested route:
- GET /admin/transactions (or /transactions if broker-facing)

Features:
- Pagination (limit + cursor/offset)
- Optional filters: client_id, tx_status, tx_type, currency, occurred_at range

TDD:
- Add storage-backed handler tests using pgtest container:
  - seed N transactions, verify pagination + ordering
  - verify filters work
- Ensure auth applies (NOT a public route).

Note: keep response DTO stable and avoid exposing raw JSON by default.
acceptance_criteria: - Endpoint returns transactions with stable ordering + pagination\n- Filters work as specified\n- Covered by integration tests

title: Docs: configure Crassula Admin Panel webhooks for transaction.*

title: Align order/settlement statuses with Linear (READY_TO_EXECUTE, SETTLING, SETTLED)
description:
Context
- Linear spec (FCE-106/FCE-109/FCE-110) defines the broker OTC happy path:
  PENDING_APPROVAL -> READY_TO_EXECUTE -> EXECUTING -> SETTLING -> SETTLED
- Canonical v1 write-up: docs/plans/2026-01-13-otc-v1-canonical-flow-design.md

Current divergence
- internal/domain/order/status.go uses READY_FOR_BROKER and COMPLETED.
- internal/domain/settlement/* uses COMPLETED/CRYPTO_SETTLED/FIAT_SETTLED, while Linear UI copy and status names use SETTLING/SETTLED.

Goal
- Make status names and transitions match the Linear-defined semantics for v1, to reduce confusion and prevent premature “COMPLETED”.

Proposed approach
- Rename order READY_FOR_BROKER -> READY_TO_EXECUTE.
- Add order SETTLING and final SETTLED (replace COMPLETED).
- Keep quote request/selection as Quote records + audit events (not extra Order statuses).
- Decide whether settlement’s final state should also be SETTLED, while keeping leg-level statuses internally.

Acceptance criteria
- Order statuses exactly match canonical set (including terminal statuses like REJECTED/ON_HOLD/etc).
- Order transition tests updated and passing.
- HTTP API returns the new status names.
- DB migration path decided (rename vs mapping) and documented.

title: Fix Crassula webhook correlation: update settlement by transaction ID (not order.crassula_ref)
description:
Problem
- internal/infrastructure/http/webhooks/handlers.go handles Crassula transaction events and uses:
  orderRepo.GetByCrassulaRef(ctx, txID)
  where txID = webhook Body.Data.ID.
- This assumes Order.crassula_ref == Crassula transaction ID, which is unlikely to be true for real transfer tracking.

Expected model
- When we initiate a crypto transfer via Crassula API, we must persist Crassula transaction ID (and/or txHash) on the Settlement (crypto leg) record.
- Webhooks should look up the Settlement/Transfer by that stored transaction ID, then update:
  - settlement crypto leg status (+ txHash)
  - derived Order status (SETTLING/SETTLED)

Acceptance criteria
- Webhook handlers correlate events using persisted transaction IDs.
- Unknown txIDs produce a safe “not found” log + 2xx response (no retries storm).
- A completed crypto webhook updates the correct settlement record and order.

title: Create rate_history table with TimescaleDB
description:
Implement time-series storage for LP rate history.

**From architecture comparison with fce-crassula-integration**

Schema:
- time (TIMESTAMPTZ, primary)
- lp_id (UUID)
- base_currency, quote_currency (VARCHAR)
- bid_rate, ask_rate, mid_rate (DECIMAL)
- spread_bps (INTEGER)
- available_amount (DECIMAL)
- source (API/WEBSOCKET/MANUAL)
- raw_response (JSONB)

TimescaleDB features:
- create_hypertable() for time partitioning
- add_retention_policy() for 8-year retention
- Compression for old data

References:
- .serena/memories/architecture-comparison-analysis.md
- fce-crassula-integration/docs/architecture/05-compliance.md
